title: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
summary: How to connect Hermes Agent to external tool servers via Model Context Protocol (MCP), including stdio and HTTP server types, per-server tool filtering, dynamic tool discovery, MCP sampling support, and running Hermes itself as an MCP server.

cli_commands.purpose: Start Hermes as an MCP server (stdio)
Start MCP server with debug logging
Add an MCP server to config

config_keys.description: Executable for a stdio MCP server
Environment variables passed to stdio server
If false, Hermes skips this server entirely
Whitelist of tool names to register from this server
Blacklist of tool names to exclude
Whether to register resource utility wrappers
Whether to register prompt utility wrappers
Enable MCP sampling (server requesting LLM inference)
Rate limit for sampling requests per minute

slash_commands.purpose: Reload MCP servers from config and refresh tool list

code_examples.caption: GitHub with whitelist filter
code_examples.code:
mcp_servers:
  github:
    command: "npx"
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    env:
      GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN: "***"
    tools:
      include: [create_issue, list_issues, search_code]
      prompts: false

code_examples.caption: MCP sampling config
code_examples.code:
mcp_servers:
  my_server:
    command: "my-mcp-server"
    sampling:
      enabled: true
      max_tokens_cap: 4096
      timeout: 30
      max_rpm: 10

code_examples.caption: Hermes as MCP server in Claude Code config

gotchas: MCP tools are prefixed as mcp_<server_name>_<tool_name> to avoid naming collisions
Resource and prompt utility tools only register if the MCP session actually supports those capabilities
If include and exclude are both set, include wins
Stdio servers only receive explicitly configured env vars plus a safe baseline — not the full shell environment
hermes mcp serve is stdio-only (no HTTP MCP transport yet)
Dynamic tool discovery via notifications/tools/list_changed is lock-protected to prevent overlapping refreshes
full_text:
# MCP (Model Context Protocol)

MCP lets Hermes Agent connect to external tool servers so the agent can use tools that live outside Hermes itself — GitHub, databases, file systems, browser stacks, internal APIs, and more.

[...full content as read from source...]
